content: The kiln at the community center is humming, a low, electric drone that vibrates through the soles of my shoes. It is Thursday evening. Outside, the Great Highway is disappearing. I watched it happen from the window—the way the white wall moves in from the Pacific, erasing the dunes, then the pavement, then the streetlamps, until there is nothing left but the amber glow of the "Don't Walk" sign at
